headline: UCLA's passing game overwhelms USC in lopsided Pac rivalry clash
body: Nico Iamaleava turned Week 5 into a masterclass in efficient quarterback play, completing 16 of 31 passes for 337 yards and three touchdowns as UCLA dismantled USC 38–9 in a contest that never felt competitive after the opening quarter. The Bruins' offense moved with surgical precision, leaning heavily on the deep ball and vertical spacing to exploit a USC secondary that never found its rhythm. Iamaleava's three scoring strikes came without forcing a single turnover, a composure that stood in stark contrast to the Trojans' labored offensive effort.

The story of this game lived in the air. USC quarterback Moss completed 19 passes for 207 yards but managed zero touchdowns while consuming significant clock without advancing the football into the end zone consistently. His receivers, led by Mack's six-catch, 59-yard showing, couldn't create separation against UCLA's coverage, and the ground game—producing just 126 yards—offered no relief valve. UCLA's rushing attack, though modest at 121 yards, never needed to impose itself because Iamaleava's arm was doing enough damage.

Harris became the focal point of Iamaleava's assault, catching six passes for 129 yards and two scores. The Bruins' defense, anchored by Johnson's six tackles, limited USC to field goals and prevented any sustained drives. For USC, this was a performance that raised questions about rhythm and execution in the passing game at a moment when the team needed to prove it could compete in the air. The Trojans' zero-turnover evening provided some operational cleanliness, but it couldn't mask the gulf in offensive efficiency across the board.
turningPoint: Harris's second touchdown reception in the second quarter gave UCLA a 21–3 cushion, and USC never manufactured the explosive plays needed to close a gap that only widened from there.

unsungHero.name: Johnson
unsungHero.why: While Iamaleava's arm made the highlight reel, Johnson's six tackles anchored a defense that forced USC into predictable third-down situations and prevented any offensive momentum that could have made this competitive.
